title: Padmanabhapuram Palace
tagline: Padmanabhapuram Palace — heritage city of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Padmanabhapuram Palace, also known as Kalkulam Palace, is a Travancore-era palace located in Padmanabhapuram in the Kanyakumari district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.
overview.description: Padmanabhapuram Palace, also known as Kalkulam Palace, is a Travancore-era palace located in Padmanabhapuram in the Kanyakumari district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The palace is owned, controlled and maintained by the Kerala Government. Padmanabhapuram is the former capital city of the erstwhile kingdom of Travancore. It is around 20 km (12 mi) from Nagercoil, 39 km (24 mi) from Kanyakumari town and 52 km (32 mi) from Thiruvananthapuram in Kerala.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Puliyoorkurichi
topPlaces.description: Puliyoorkurichi (Tamil: புலியூர்குறிச்சி, also transliterated as Puliyurkurichi or Puliurkuruchi) is a small village in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is a suburb of Padmanabhapuram, about 3 kilometres (1.9 mi) southeast of Thuckalay on the Trivandrum-Nagercoil Highway.

topPlaces.name: Peer Mohammed Dargah
topPlaces.description: Peer Mohamed Oliyullah Dargha is the Dargah of Sufi philosopher, and Tamil poet Peer Mohamed Appa located 35 km from Thuckalay. It is one of the most renowned Sufi pilgrim centers in Kanyakumari, the southernmost district of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Padmanabhapuram
topPlaces.description: Padmanabhapuram (Malayalam: [pɐd̪mɐnaːbʰɐpuɾɐm]) is a town near Thuckalay in Kanyakumari district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. As of 2011, the town had a population of 21,342.

topPlaces.name: Thuckalay
topPlaces.description: Thuckalay or Thakkalai is a town in Kanyakumari district of Tamil Nadu, India. Thuckalay comes under the Padmanabhapuram Municipality, and is the administrative headquarters of Kalkulam Taluk.

topPlaces.name: Mandaikadu
topPlaces.description: Mandaikadu is a coastal panchayat town in Kanniyakumari district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The town is known for its ten day long annual Koda festival that attracts hundreds of devotees.

topPlaces.name: Kumarakovil
topPlaces.description: Kumarakoil or Kumarakovil is a small village located in the Kanyakumari district in Tamil Nadu, India. It is the site of an important Murugan temple of the same name.

topPlaces.name: Noorul Islam Centre for Higher Education
topPlaces.description: Noorul Islam Centre for Higher Education (NICHE), formerly Noorul Islam College of Engineering, is a private co-educational Institution in Kumarakovil, Thuckalay, Kanyakumari District, Tamil Nadu, India. The institution was founded in 1989 by A.P. Majeed Khan and it was declared as a Deemed to be University by the Ministry of Human Resource Development, Govt.

image.alt: Noorul Islam Centre for Higher Education

topPlaces.name: Vilavur
topPlaces.description: Vilavur is a panchayat town in Kanniyakumari district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.Nearest Airport Thiruvananthapuram(KERALA)

items.note: Puliyoorkurichi (Tamil: புலியூர்குறிச்சி, also transliterated as Puliyurkurichi or Puliurkuruchi) is a small village in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is a suburb of Padmanabhapuram, about 3 kilometres (1.9 mi) southeast of Thuckalay on the Trivandrum-Nagercoil Highway.

Peer Mohamed Oliyullah Dargha is the Dargah of Sufi philosopher, and Tamil poet Peer Mohamed Appa located 35 km from Thuckalay. It is one of the most renowned Sufi pilgrim centers in Kanyakumari, the southernmost district of Tamil Nadu.
Padmanabhapuram (Malayalam: [pɐd̪mɐnaːbʰɐpuɾɐm]) is a town near Thuckalay in Kanyakumari district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. As of 2011, the town had a population of 21,342.
Thuckalay or Thakkalai is a town in Kanyakumari district of Tamil Nadu, India. Thuckalay comes under the Padmanabhapuram Municipality, and is the administrative headquarters of Kalkulam Taluk.
Mandaikadu is a coastal panchayat town in Kanniyakumari district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The town is known for its ten day long annual Koda festival that attracts hundreds of devotees.
Kumarakoil or Kumarakovil is a small village located in the Kanyakumari district in Tamil Nadu, India. It is the site of an important Murugan temple of the same name.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Padmanabhapuram Palace?
faq.a: The best time to visit Padmanabhapuram Palace is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Padmanabhapuram Palace?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (52 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (49 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Padmanabhapuram Palace?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Padmanabhapuram Palace at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Padmanabhapuram Palace?
faq.a: Stays in Padmanabhapuram Palace start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Padmanabhapuram Palace?
faq.a: The top attractions are Puliyoorkurichi, Peer Mohammed Dargah, Padmanabhapuram, Thuckalay, Mandaikadu.
